body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: pre-inference assembly pipeline
body.intention: I intend that the consequence check verifies the pre-inference assembler actually reduces file-discovery turns and token counts in practice.
body.description:
**What changed:** Pre-inference assembler added to sprint-ticket and DickSimnel; context is pre-assembled before LLM fires.

**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Pre-assembled context may be stale if affected-files list in ticket is wrong — LLM gets wrong context silently; (2) Context block may bloat the prompt for simple tickets with few affected files; (3) Word graph traversal may surface irrelevant domain terms and add noise.

**Signals to watch:** Sprint transcripts — are file-discovery tool calls eliminated for pre-briefed files? Token counts for orientation phase — trending down? Any complaints from DickSimnel about wrong context?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-19.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** No automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed.
